name: kubernetes-architect
description: Expert Kubernetes architect specializing in cloud-native
capabilities: Design multi-cluster Kubernetes architectures for EKS, AKS, GKE, and on-premises deployments
Implement GitOps workflows using ArgoCD, Flux v2, and progressive delivery patterns
Configure service mesh solutions including Istio, Linkerd, and Cilium for traffic management
Define security policies with Pod Security Standards, network policies, and OPA/Gatekeeper
Set up observability stacks with Prometheus, Grafana, Fluentd, and OpenTelemetry
Optimize costs and resources with autoscaling, right-sizing, and FinOps practices
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing no executable code. Static analysis scanned 1 file (SKILL.md) with 173 lines and found zero security risks. The skill provides Kubernetes architecture guidance through text-based prompts and instructions only. No network access, file operations, external commands, or code execution patterns detected.
valueStatement: Building production Kubernetes infrastructure is complex and error-prone. This skill provides expert guidance on cluster architecture, GitOps workflows, and enterprise security patterns to accelerate your platform engineering.
userTitle: Design Kubernetes platforms with GitOps and cloud-native patterns
limitations: Cannot execute kubectl commands or apply configurations to actual clusters
Cannot access external systems, cloud providers, or container registries directly
Recommendations require validation against your specific environment constraints
Does not replace the need for security audits and compliance reviews

useCases.targetUser: Platform engineers building internal developer platforms
useCases.title: Enterprise Platform Design
useCases.description: Design a multi-tenant Kubernetes platform with namespace isolation, RBAC, and GitOps workflows for a mid-size technology company migrating from VMs to containers.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps teams adopting continuous deployment practices
useCases.title: GitOps Implementation
useCases.description: Implement ArgoCD-based GitOps with progressive delivery, automated testing, and environment promotion strategies for microservices deployments.

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers responsible for container platform governance
useCases.title: Security Hardening
useCases.description: Implement comprehensive Kubernetes security including Pod Security Standards, network policies, image scanning, and admission controllers for compliance requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cluster Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to deploy a Node.js microservices application to Kubernetes. Help me design a basic cluster architecture with appropriate namespaces, resource quotas, and deployment strategies for a development environment.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a Kubernetes migration for a small team

promptTemplates.title: GitOps Workflow Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a GitOps workflow using ArgoCD for our organization. We have three environments (dev, staging, production) and need automated deployments with manual approval gates. Include repository structure, sync policies, and rollback procedures.
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing continuous deployment with governance controls

promptTemplates.title: Service Mesh Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: We are experiencing intermittent service-to-service communication issues in our microservices architecture. Recommend a service mesh solution and provide configuration for traffic management, mutual TLS, and observability. Compare Istio and Linkerd for our use case.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Cluster Disaster Recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a disaster recovery strategy for our financial services Kubernetes platform. We require RTO of 15 minutes and RPO of 5 minutes across two regions. Include backup strategies, failover procedures, and testing recommendations using Velero and traffic management.

bestPractices: Always implement GitOps from project inception rather than retrofitting into existing workflows
Use namespace-based multi-tenancy with network policies for workload isolation
Configure resource requests and limits based on actual usage metrics, not estimates
Implement defense in depth with pod security standards, network policies, and admission controllers
Set up comprehensive observability before deploying production workloads
antiPatterns: Running containers as root or with privileged security contexts
Managing Kubernetes resources manually with kubectl instead of GitOps
Using latest tags for container images in production deployments
Skipping staging validation before applying changes to production clusters
Ignoring resource limits leading to noisy neighbor problems

faq.question: Which Kubernetes providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers all major managed Kubernetes services including EKS (AWS), AKS (Azure), GKE (Google Cloud), as well as self-managed clusters using kubeadm, kops, Rancher, and OpenShift.

faq.question: Can this skill apply configurations to my actual cluster?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides architecture guidance and configuration recommendations only. You must review and apply any configurations to your clusters manually after appropriate validation.

faq.question: What GitOps tools are covered?
faq.answer: Primary coverage includes ArgoCD and Flux v2, with additional guidance on Jenkins X, Tekton pipelines, and integration with CI/CD systems like GitHub Actions and GitLab CI.

faq.question: Does this skill help with cost optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers resource right-sizing, spot instance strategies, cluster autoscaling, KubeCost/OpenCost implementation, and workload placement optimization for cost efficiency.

faq.question: What security frameworks does this skill address?
faq.answer: Coverage includes Pod Security Standards, CIS Kubernetes Benchmarks, NIST container security guidelines, supply chain security with SLSA and Sigstore, and compliance automation patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill help with service mesh selection?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides comparative analysis of Istio, Linkerd, Cilium, and Consul Connect with guidance on traffic management, security policies, observability integration, and operational complexity trade-offs.
